messages.content: In diraclang, if num is greater than 10, print 'Value is large'; otherwise, print 'Value is small'.

<dirac>  <defvar name="num" value="5" />  <test-if test="$num > 10">    <output>Value is large</output>  </test-if>  <test-if test="$num <= 10">    <output>Value is small</output>  </test-if> </dirac>
In diraclang, loop from 0 to 2 and print each value.
<dirac>  <defvar name="i" value="0" />  <loop count="3">    <output><variable name="i" /></output>    <assign name="i"><eval><variable name="i" /> + 1</eval></assign>  </loop> </dirac>
In diraclang, define a variable myvar with value 'test123' and print it.
In diraclang, define variables a=10, b=5, and print their sum as 'Result: 15'.

<dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="10" />  <defvar name="b" value="5" />  <output>Result: </output>  <expr eval="plus">    <arg><variable name="a" /></arg>    <arg><variable name="b" /></arg>  </expr> </dirac>
In diraclang, define a variable x=5, use an if conditional to check if x equals 5, print 'Match!' if true, 'No match!' if false.
In diraclang, throw an error and catch it, then print 'Caught error'.
In diraclang, write two lines to a file, output to stdout, verify file and clean up.

<dirac>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 1</output>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 2</output>  <output>stdout output</output>  <system shell="bash">    if [ -f output-test-temp.txt ]; then      echo "File written"      rm output-test-temp.txt    fi  </system> </dirac>
In diraclang, import a math library and call SQUARE with x=5.
In diraclang, define a subroutine fullname with first and last name, and print 'John Doe'.

<dirac>  <subroutine name="fullname" param-first="string" param-last="string">    <eval name="result">return first + ' ' + last;</eval>    <output><variable name="result" /></output>  </subroutine>    <call name="fullname" first="John" last="Doe" /> </dirac>
In diraclang, define a parent subroutine without visible, call nested, and print cleanup message.

<subroutine name="PARENT_WITHOUT_VISIBLE">  <output>Parent without visible called</output>    <subroutine name="SHOULD_BE_CLEANED">    <output>This should not be called</output>  </subroutine> </subroutine>  <PARENT_WITHOUT_VISIBLE />  <output>After call: nested was cleaned up</output>
In diraclang, print 'Hello World' without a root element.
In diraclang, generate a script, write to file, execute, and print result.

<dirac>  <defvar name="generated_script" literal="true" trim="false">
<dirac>  <defvar name="value">42</defvar>  <output>Result: <variable name="value" /></output>
</dirac>  </defvar>  <output file="temp-generated.di"><variable name="generated_script" /></output>  <output>Generated file executed</output>  <system shell="bash">    node dist/cli.js temp-generated.di    rm temp-generated.di  </system> </dirac>
In diraclang, echo hello using system command.
In diraclang, import a library with variable substitution and double 10.

<dirac>  <subroutine name="TestSuite">    <defvar name="libPath">./fixtures/test-lib.di</defvar>    <import src="${libPath}" />    <defvar name="result" trim="true"><DOUBLE x="10" /></defvar>    <output><variable name="result" /></output>  </subroutine>  <TestSuite /> </dirac>
In diraclang, set background color to blue using llm and validate.

<dirac>  <subroutine name="set-color" description="Set a background color" param-color="string:required:Color name">    <parameters select="@color"/>    <output>Background color is: <variable name="color" /></output>  </subroutine>  <llm execute="true" validate="true" maxTokens="100">    Set the background color to blue  </llm> </dirac>
In diraclang, break a loop at 3 and print after loop.

<loop count="10">  <test-if test="$i" ge="3">    <output>Breaking at <variable name="i" /></output>    <break />  </test-if>  <output>Loop: <variable name="i" /></output> </loop> <output>After loop</output>
In diraclang, check if PATH environment variable exists and print result.
In diraclang, read all stdin and print it.
In diraclang, test if x is greater than 5 and print 'Greater!'.
In diraclang, Throw an error and catch it, then print 'Caught error'.
In diraclang, Create a nested loop: for i in 0..1 and j in 0..1, print i and letter A or B depending on j.

<dirac>  <loop count="2" var="i">    <loop count="2" var="j">      <eval name="letter">return j === 0 ? 'A' : 'B';</eval>      <output><variable name="i" />-<variable name="letter" /> </output>    </loop>  </loop> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define parent and child subroutines, child extends parent and overrides nested, call child and print outputs.

<dirac>  <subroutine name="parent">    <subroutine name="nested">      <output>Parent nested</output>    </subroutine>    <output>In parent, calling: </output>    <call name="nested" />  </subroutine>    <subroutine name="child" extends="parent">    <subroutine name="nested">      <output>Child nested</output>    </subroutine>  </subroutine>    <call name="child" /> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define a TestSuite subroutine with age, gender, greet, and use available-subroutines to list them.

<dirac> <subroutine name="TestSuite">  <subroutine name="age" description="Returns the person age">    <output>42</output>  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="gender" description="Returns gender identity">    <output>Male</output>  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="greet" description="Greets someone">    <output>Hello!</output>  </subroutine>  <available-subroutines /> </subroutine> <TestSuite /> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define variables a=10, b=20, use eval to sum them, and print 'Sum: 30'.
<dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="10" />  <defvar name="b" value="20" />  <eval name="sum">return parseInt(a) + parseInt(b);</eval>  <output>Sum: <variable name="sum" /> </output> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Assign a variable myvar to 'initial', update it to 'updated', and print 'New value: updated'.
<dirac>  <defvar name="myvar" value="initial" />  <assign name="myvar" value="updated" />  <output>New value: <variable name="myvar" /> </output> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define variables a=1, b=2, c=3 and print them as 'a=1, b=2, c=3'.

<dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="1" />  <defvar name="b" value="2" />  <defvar name="c" value="3" />  <output>a=<variable name="a" />, b=<variable name="b" />, c=<variable name="c" /></output> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Loop three times, double the index, and print each doubled value.
In diraclang, Write two lines to a file, output to stdout, verify file and clean up.

<dirac>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 1</output>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 2</output>  <output>stdout output</output>  <system shell="bash">    if [ -f output-test-temp.txt ]; then      echo "File written"      rm output-test-temp.txt    fi  </system> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Loop from 0 to 2 and print each value.
<dirac>  <defvar name="i" value="0" />  <loop count="3">    <output><variable name="i" /></output>    <assign name="i"><eval><variable name="i" /> + 1</eval></assign>  </loop> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define a variable x=5, use an if conditional to check if x equals 5, print 'Match!' if true, 'No match!' if false.
In diraclang, Define a parent subroutine with visible attribute, register a nested helper subroutine, and call the parent to print before, nested, after, and call helper.

<subroutine name="PARENT_WITH_VISIBLE" visible="subroutine">  <output>Before call</output>    <subroutine name="NESTED_HELPER">    <output>Calling NESTED_HELPER: Success!</output>  </subroutine>    <output>Nested registered</output> </subroutine><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Assign a variable result to 0, use eval to set result to 10+5, and print 'Result: 15'.
In diraclang, Define inner and outer subroutines, call outer with p=x and q=y, and print main scope variables before and after.

<dirac>  <subroutine name="inner" param-r="string" param-s="string">    <output>inner: r=<variable name="r" /> s=<variable name="s" /></output>  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="outer" param-p="string" param-q="string">    <output>outer: p=<variable name="p" /> q=<variable name="q" /></output>    <inner r="$p" s="$q" />    <output>outer after inner: p=<variable name="p" /> q=<variable name="q" /></output>  </subroutine>  <defvar name="a">x</defvar>  <defvar name="b">y</defvar>  <defvar name="c">z</defvar>  <output>main: a=<variable name="a" /> b=<variable name="b" /> c=<variable name="c" /></output>  <outer p="$a" q="$b" />  <output>main after outer: a=<variable name="a" /> b=<variable name="b" /> c=<variable name="c" /></output> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define base subroutine with age and gender, extend base to override age, and print age and gender.

<dirac>  <subroutine name="base">    <subroutine name="age">      <output>60</output>    </subroutine>    <subroutine name="gender">      <output>M</output>    </subroutine>    <parameters select="*" />  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="base" extends="base">    <subroutine name="age">      <output>63</output>    </subroutine>    <parameters select="*" />  </subroutine>  <base><age /></base>  <output>,</output>  <base><gender /></base> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Start a background process that counts, check file lines in a loop, verify completion, and clean up.

<dirac>  <output>Testing background processes with loop...</output>  <system>rm -f /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <system background="true">(for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do echo "Count: $i"; sleep 1; done > /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt) &</system>  <output>✓ Background counter started</output>  <output>✓ DIRAC continuing without blocking</output>  <system>sleep 2</system>  <loop count="3">    <system>wc -l /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "0"</system>    <output>Check: Background file now has lines</output>    <system>sleep 1</system>  </loop>  <system>sleep 2</system>  <system>wc -l /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <output>✓ Background process completed</output>  <system>grep -c "Count:" /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <output>✓ Found all count entries</output>  <system>rm -f /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <output>✓ Background process with loop test passed</output> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Define a variable 'status' as 'active' and print 'Match found' if status is active.

Dirac is a declarative execution language designed for the AI era, where large language models act as autonomous agents. It bridges human-readable instructions with machine execution, allowing LLMs to generate, execute, and recursively call code for complex tasks.<|eot_id|>
In diraclang, If num is greater than 10, print 'Value is large'; otherwise, print 'Value is small'.

<dirac>  <defvar name="num" value="5" />  <test-if test="$num > 10">    <output>Value is large</output>  </test-if>  <test-if test="$num <= 10">    <output>Value is small</output>  </test-if> </dirac><|eot_id|>

In diraclang, call defvar with parameters: name (Variable name), value (Initial value (or use children/text)), visible (Visibility flag|true|false|variable|both), literal (Store children as literal XML without execution), trim (Trim whitespace from value|true|false).

In diraclang, call list-subroutines with parameters: format (Output format (default), output (Variable name to store result).
In diraclang, Save subroutine definition to disk.
In diraclang, use save-subroutine with name: Subroutine name to save.
In diraclang, call save-subroutine with parameters: name (Subroutine name to save), file (Explicit file path), path (Directory name under ~/.dirac/lib/), format (Output format (default).
In diraclang, Search and manage subroutine knowledge base.
<dirac>  <subroutine-index path="/path/to/dir" query="search term" limit="limit" output="output" format="Hello World" /> </dirac><|eot_id|>

In diraclang, call subroutine-index with parameters: path (Directory to index or search), query (Natural language search query), limit (Maximum results to return), output (Variable name to store results), format (Output format|text|json).
<dirac>  <subroutine-index path="/path/to/dir" query="search term" limit="limit" output="output" format="Hello World" /> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Execute dynamically generated DIRAC code.
In diraclang, call execute with parameters: source (Variable containing DIRAC code).
In diraclang, call system with parameters: background (Run in background without waiting|true|false).
In diraclang, Import subroutines from other DIRAC files.
In diraclang, use import with src: File path or package name.
In diraclang, call import with parameters: src (File path or package name).
In diraclang, Load Node.js module into variable.
In diraclang, use require_module with name: Module name to import.
In diraclang, call require_module with parameters: name (Module name to import), var (Variable name to store module (defaults to name)).
In diraclang, Invoke Large Language Model with validation.

<dirac>  <llm model="model" output="output" context="Hello World" save-dialog="save-dialog" execute="execute" temperature="temperature" maxTokens="token_abc" noextra="Hello World" validate="123" autocorrect="123" max-retries="123" feedback="123" max-iterations="max-iterations" replace-tick="replace-tick" /> </dirac><|eot_id|>

In diraclang, call llm with parameters: model (Model name or use DEFAULT_MODEL env var), output (Variable to store result), context (Context variable for RAG), save-dialog (Enable dialog persistence|true|false), execute (Execute generated code immediately|true|false), temperature (Sampling temperature (default), maxTokens (Maximum tokens to generate (default), noextra (Skip extra context injection|true|false), validate (Validate generated DIRAC tags|true|false), autocorrect (Auto-fix validation errors|true|false), max-retries (Validation retry attempts (default), feedback (Enable validation feedback loop|true|false), max-iterations (Max feedback iterations (default), replace-tick (Replace backticks in code|true|false).

<dirac>  <llm model="model" output="output" context="Hello World" save-dialog="save-dialog" execute="execute" temperature="temperature" maxTokens="token_abc" noextra="Hello World" validate="123" autocorrect="123" max-retries="123" feedback="123" max-iterations="max-iterations" replace-tick="replace-tick" /> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, Load subroutine definitions as LLM context.
In diraclang, call load-context with parameters: limit (Maximum subroutines to include), import (Also import subroutines (default), output (Variable to store context text).

In diraclang, Run tasks on interval without blocking.
In diraclang, use schedule with interval: Seconds between executions.
In diraclang, call schedule with parameters: interval (Seconds between executions), name (Task identifier for logging).
<dirac>  <mongodb connection="connection" database="mydb" collection="users" operation="operation" query="search term" document="document" output="output" /> </dirac><|eot_id|>
In diraclang, use mongodb with connection: MongoDB connection string, database: Database name, collection: Collection name, operation: Operation to perform|insert|find|update|delete.
